sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) [In Thes. from לוּשׁ], a lion, so called from his strength, Isa 30:6 Job 4:11 Pro 30:30. (Arab. لَيْثُ, Chald. לַיִת, Gr. λῖς, ap. Hom. id.)

(2) [Laish], pr.n.-(a) of a place on the northern borders of Palestine, elsewhere called לֶשֶׁם and דָּן (which see), Jud. 18:7 , 29 Jdg 18:29with the addition of ־ָה local, Isa 10:30, where others understand some other town near Jerusalem to be intended; see Comment. on the place.-(b) of a man, 1Sa 25:44, and 2Sa 3:15 קרי.
